title: Matelea oxypetaloides
taxonomy: Matelea oxypetaloides is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a part of the order Gentianales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Gentianales is a part of the clade asterids, one of the two main clades within the angiosperms. The clade asterids is further divided into several subclasses, and the subclass Asteridae is where the order Gentianales is classified. The subclass Asteridae is a part of the larger clade eudicots, which includes the majority of flowering plant species.
uses: Matelea oxypetaloides is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. In some regions, its extracts are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's properties are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Additionally, Matelea oxypetaloides is sometimes used as a natural remedy for digestive problems and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.

side_effects: Matelea oxypetaloides, also known as the "swallow-wort" or "dog's-teeth," has been reported to have toxic properties if ingested. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or stems can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in animals and humans. The plant's toxic compounds can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. intro: Matelea oxypetaloides, also known as the "Coral Milkflower," is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant effects, contributing to the treatment of various health issues such as muscle spasms and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Matelea oxypetaloides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to relieve muscle pain and promote relaxation.
